seoTitle: How to Quote in Google Docs: Add Citations Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to insert block quotes and automatic citations in Google Docs using the built-in Citations tool for APA, MLA, or Chicago style.
intro: Adding properly formatted quotes and citations to a Google Doc doesn't have to mean manually indenting paragraphs or typing out bibliography entries by hand. Google Docs has a built-in Citations tool that handles block quote indentation and generates parenthetical references automatically. This guide walks through setting up a quote, choosing a citation style, and building a source list.

steps.heading: Select your quote text
steps.body: Highlight the passage you want to use as a block quote, then drag the ruler's left indent handle inward to visually separate it from the surrounding paragraph.

steps.heading: Open the Citations tool
steps.body: Go to Tools > Citations in the top menu and choose your preferred style guide, such as APA, MLA, or Chicago.

steps.heading: Add a citation source
steps.body: Click 'Add citation source,' pick the source type (web article, book, or journal), and fill in details like author, title, and publication date.

steps.heading: Insert the inline citation
steps.body: Place your cursor at the end of the quoted text, hover over the saved source in the sidebar, and click 'Cite' to auto-insert the correct reference tag.

steps.heading: Generate the bibliography
steps.body: Scroll to the end of your document and click 'Insert bibliography' to automatically render a formatted source list from everything you've cited.

faq.question: Can I switch citation styles after adding sources?
faq.answer: Yes, you can change the style selection in the Citations panel and Google Docs will reformat your existing citations and bibliography accordingly.

faq.question: Does the bibliography update automatically if I add more sources?
faq.answer: You need to re-insert or refresh the bibliography after adding new citations so it reflects the latest source list.

faq.question: Is the Citations tool free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, it's a built-in feature of Google Docs available to any Google account holder at no extra cost.

faq.question: Can I manually edit an inserted citation?
faq.answer: Yes, inline citations and bibliography entries are regular text once inserted, so you can adjust formatting or wording as needed.
